commitMessage:
Impl #2402 - Migrate model processors from extension point to OSGi DS

Signed-off-by: Dirk Fauth <dirk.fauth@googlemail.com>
categoryReason: The changes involve migrating to OSGi Declarative Services, which is a specific structural and architectural change (adding component annotations, implementing interfaces, injecting references) not covered by existing general 'Java-Modernization' or other categories.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would require significant extensions to support: 1. Adding new class-level annotations with complex attribute values (e.g., array of class literals like `service = { IModelProcessorContribution.class, CommandToModelProcessor.class }`). 2. Modifying class declaration `implements` clauses. 3. Inserting new field declarations (including their annotations) into a class.
summary: This commit migrates model processors to OSGi Declarative Services. The changes include adding `@Component` annotations to class declarations, modifying class signatures to `implement IModelProcessorContribution`, and injecting dependencies via new `@Reference` fields. These are structural modifications (adding annotations to declaration, changing `implements` clause, inserting new fields) that are not currently supported by the TriggerPattern DSL. This would require substantial language extensions for arbitrary code insertion and structural definition changes.
